evals.prompt: Add an HTTP REST connector to my process that fetches user data from https://api.example.com/users/{userId}. The userId comes from a process variable.
evals.expected_output: BPMN with HTTP connector applied via element-templates-cli, configured with GET method and dynamic URL
expectations: Runs the extract-templates.js script or references cached templates
Uses npx element-templates-cli to apply the http-json-connector template
Configures input mappings with method=GET and a FEEL expression for the URL
Sets resultVariable or resultExpression for the response
Uses proper XML encoding for FEEL expressions in attributes

evals.prompt: What connectors are available for sending messages or notifications?
evals.expected_output: List of messaging-related connectors from the element templates
expectations: Identifies messaging connectors like Slack, SendGrid, email, RabbitMQ, Kafka
Provides brief description of what each connector does

evals.prompt: I have a service task Task_SendSlack in my process. Configure it to send a Slack message to the #alerts channel with the incident details.
evals.expected_output: Applies Slack connector template and configures channel and message properties
expectations: Uses element-templates-cli to apply the Slack outbound connector template
Reads the template JSON to understand required properties
Configures channel and message input mappings
Uses secrets reference for the Slack token
